title: Walt Disney on the making of Snow White: CBC Archives
text:
how long was Snow White in production
world I actually started to uh plan the
picture about
1935 and I fool around with it trying to
get a hold of a story and things for
couple of years and finally it begin to
jail then I went to work on it I
finished it the fall of
1938 I didn't know what I had or what
would happen or anything we had the the
family fortune we had everything wrapped
up in know white in fact the the banker
I think was losing more sleep than I was
and fortunately they though we put it in
premiered it and everything else why
everything was was fine the banker was
happy and the following spring Along
Came that Academy Award oh yeah it
wasn't but about two years later that I
was almost broke well two years later
was following Pinocchio and coming into
the Fantasia period yeah that's about
did it you know that but that was a new
artistic success Financial failure you
know certainly an artistic success it
was a magn well some people would uh
would question that too Mickey was
certainly a mouse of all trades and
particularly impressive as a conductor
well that's what started I was doing
this sources Apprentice with Mickey
Mouse and I happened to have dinner one
night with
stovi and stovi said oh I would love to
conduct that for you you know well that
was led to not only doing this one
little short subject but it got us
involved where I did all of Fantasia and
before I knew it I ended up spending
$400 some odd thousand getting music
with spikowski
well perfectionism always costs money I
guess that's something you've always
been after well I was always known as
the perfectionist until I met
stoski and you had a new member of the
club indeed tell us Walt what are the
principal technical advances in the
animated cartoons since the time of
Fantasia better
drawing uh the first thing I did when I
got a little money to experiment I put
all my artists back in
school we uh uh art schools that existed
then didn't quite have enough uh for
what we needed so we set up our own art
school well you were inventing a new art
anyway well yes but we were just going a
little bit beyond what they were getting
in the art school where they work with
the static figure now we were dealing in
in motion movement and the flow of
movement the flow of things you know
Action Reaction all of that so we had to
set up our own school and out of that
school had come the the artists that now
make up my staff here and more than that
the artists that make up all of the most
all of the cartoon outfits in Hollywood
mm where directly or indirectly out of
my school
